testcase.name: test_byte_order_mark
testcase.failure:
self = <test.test_sectxt.SecTxtTestCase testMethod=test_byte_order_mark>
def test_byte_order_mark(self): with Mocker() as m: expires = f"Expires: {(date.today() + timedelta(days=10)).isoformat()}T18:37:07z\n" byte_content_with_bom = b'\xef\xbb\xbf\xef\xbb\xbfContact: mailto:me@example.com\n' \ + expires.encode() m.get( "https://example.com/.well-known/security.txt", headers={"content-type": "text/plain"}, content=byte_content_with_bom, ) > s = SecurityTXT("example.com")
test/test_sectxt.py:355: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ sectxt/__init__.py:544: in __init__ super().__init__(b'', recommend_unknown_fields=recommend_unknown_fields, is_local=is_local) sectxt/__init__.py:91: in __init__ self._process() _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <sectxt.SecurityTXT object at 0x111d3f770>
def _process(self) -> None: if self.is_local: security_txt_file = open(self._loc, mode="rb") self._content = security_txt_file.read() security_txt_file.close() super()._process() else: security_txt_found = False for scheme in ["https", "http"]: for path in [".well-known/security.txt", "security.txt"]: url = urlunsplit((scheme, self._loc, path, None, None)) try: resp = treq.get( # Changed from requests.get to treq.get url, headers={ 'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; WOW64; rv:12.0) ' 'Gecko/20100101 Firefox/12.0'}, timeout=5 ) except treq.exceptions.SSLError: if not any(d["code"] == "invalid_cert" for d in self._errors): self._add_error( "invalid_cert", "security.txt must be served with a valid TLS certificate.", ) try: resp = treq.get( # Changed from requests.get to treq.get url, headers={ 'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; WOW64; rv:12.0) ' 'Gecko/20100101 Firefox/12.0'}, timeout=5, verify=False ) except: continue except: continue > if resp.status_code == 200: E AttributeError: 'Deferred' object has no attribute 'status_code'
sectxt/__init__.py:584: AttributeError

testcase.name: test_invalid_uri_scheme
testcase.failure:
self = <test.test_sectxt.SecTxtTestCase testMethod=test_invalid_uri_scheme>
def test_invalid_uri_scheme(self): with Mocker() as m: m.get( "https://example.com/.well-known/security.txt", > exc=treq.exceptions.ConnectTimeout, ) E AttributeError: module 'treq' has no attribute 'exceptions'
test/test_sectxt.py:326: AttributeError

testcase.name: test_multiple_signed_messages
testcase.failure:
self = <test.test_sectxt.SecTxtTestCase testMethod=test_multiple_signed_messages>
def test_multiple_signed_messages(self): content = _signed_example.replace( "-----BEGIN PGP SIGNATURE-----", "-----BEGIN PGP SIGNATURE-----\n-----BEGIN PGP SIGNATURE-----", ) p = Parser(content.encode()) > self.assertFalse(p.is_valid()) E AssertionError: True is not false
test/test_sectxt.py:272: AssertionError

testcase.name: test_not_correct_path
testcase.failure:
self = <test.test_sectxt.SecTxtTestCase testMethod=test_not_correct_path>
def test_not_correct_path(self): with Mocker() as m: m.get( "https://example.com/.well-known/security.txt", > exc=treq.exceptions.ConnectTimeout, ) E AttributeError: module 'treq' has no attribute 'exceptions'
test/test_sectxt.py:314: AttributeError

testcase.name: test_valid_security_txt
testcase.failure:
self = <test.test_sectxt.SecTxtTestCase testMethod=test_valid_security_txt>
def test_valid_security_txt(self): with Mocker() as m: expires = f"Expires: {(date.today() + timedelta(days=10)).isoformat()}T18:37:07z\n" byte_content = b'Contact: mailto:me@example.com\n' + bytes(expires, "utf-8") m.get( "https://example.com/.well-known/security.txt", headers={"content-type": "text/plain"}, content=byte_content ) m.get("https://example.com/security.txt", text=_signed_example) > s = SecurityTXT("example.com")

self = <sectxt.SecurityTXT object at 0x111fa0910>
def _process(self) -> None: if self.is_local: security_txt_file = open(self._loc, mode="rb") self._content = security_txt_file.read() security_txt_file.close() super()._process() else: security_txt_found = False for scheme in ["https", "http"]: for path in [".well-known/security.txt", "security.txt"]: url = urlunsplit((scheme, self._loc, path, None, None)) try: resp = treq.get( # Changed from requests.get to treq.get url, headers={ 'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; WOW64; rv:12.0) ' 'Gecko/20100101 Firefox/12.0'}, timeout=5 ) except treq.exceptions.SSLError: if not any(d["code"] == "invalid_cert" for d in self._errors): self._add_error( "invalid_cert", "security.txt must be served with a valid TLS certificate.", ) try: resp = treq.get( # Changed from requests.get to treq.get url, headers={ 'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; WOW64; rv:12.0) ' 'Gecko/20100101 Firefox/12.0'}, timeout=5, verify=False ) except: continue except: continue > if resp.status_code == 200: E AttributeError: 'Deferred' object has no attribute 'status_code'
